description: Comprehensive fix of all bugs, security gaps, DB schema issues, performance bottlenecks, missing features (variations), and dead code cleanup in the Shopee integration. Diagnosed by 5 parallel audit agents on 2026-03-06.

userStories.title: Fix _get_shop_lock TOCTOU race condition
userStories.description: As a system, I want the per-shop token lock to be atomically created so that concurrent coroutines cannot create duplicate locks.
acceptanceCriteria: In `app/services/shopee_api.py`, replace the `_get_shop_lock` function body with a single line: `return _token_locks.setdefault(shop_id, asyncio.Lock())`
The function still returns an `asyncio.Lock` for the given shop_id
No other changes to the file in this story
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: One-line fix. Current code at lines 30-33 has if/set pattern that two coroutines can race through. dict.setdefault is atomic in CPython.

userStories.title: Abort copy when zero images uploaded
userStories.description: As a system, I want to fail fast when no images were uploaded successfully, instead of calling add_item 3 times with an empty image list.
acceptanceCriteria: In `app/services/shopee_copier.py`, in `copy_single_item`, after `_upload_images()` returns, check if `image_ids` is empty
If empty, return an error result immediately with message: 'Falha no upload de todas as imagens — nao e possivel criar o anuncio'
The error is recorded in the result dict with status 'error'
No `add_item` call is made when image_ids is empty
Debug log entry is written via `_log_debug` with action 'shopee_image_upload_failed'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Currently at shopee_copier.py:351-352 it logs a warning but proceeds. add_item requires at least 1 image_id. The 3 retry attempts all fail with empty images — waste of API calls. Check the return dict format used by other error paths in the same function for consistency.

userStories.title: Fix _strip_attributes to use exclusion list instead of is_mandatory
userStories.description: As a system, I want retry attempt 2 to remove only known-problematic attributes (brand, etc.) instead of all attributes, so items keep their required category attributes.
acceptanceCriteria: In `app/services/shopee_copier.py`, modify `_strip_attributes` to define a set of attribute IDs to exclude (brand-related), e.g. `STRIP_ATTR_IDS = set()` — or filter by known problematic attribute names/patterns
Instead of filtering `a.get('is_mandatory')` (which is never present), filter OUT attributes whose `attribute_id` is in the exclusion set, or whose value contains brand-related data
As a simple heuristic: remove attributes where the attribute value list is empty or where the attribute appears to be brand (check common Shopee brand attribute IDs or names)
If after filtering the list is empty, delete the `attribute_list` key (preserve current fallback behavior)
Attributes NOT in the exclusion criteria are KEPT in the payload
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Current code at lines 286-297 filters `a.get('is_mandatory')` but this field comes from get_attributes (category API), NOT from get_item_base_info. It's never present in the source item's attribute data, so the filter always returns empty list — removing ALL attributes. A pragmatic fix: instead of trying to detect mandatory, just remove the ones known to cause issues (brand). The Shopee brand attribute has no universal ID, so the safest approach is: keep all attributes but strip attribute_list entries with empty attribute_value_list.

userStories.title: Fix updated_at on token refresh + condition from source + dead import
userStories.description: As a system, I want shopee_sellers.updated_at to be current after token refresh, item condition to come from the source, and dead imports removed.
acceptanceCriteria: In `app/services/shopee_api.py`, in the `_get_token` refresh path where token is updated in DB, add `updated_at` to the update dict with value `datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()`
In `app/services/shopee_copier.py`, in `_build_shopee_payload`, use `base.get('condition', 'NEW')` from source data instead of hardcoded `'NEW'`
In `app/routers/auth_shopee.py`, remove the unused `from urllib.parse import urlencode` import
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Three small fixes in three files. For updated_at: the refresh path in _get_token does db.table('shopee_sellers').update({...}) — just add 'updated_at' to that dict. For condition: line ~166 or ~311 in shopee_copier.py has condition='NEW' hardcoded. For urlencode: line 7 in auth_shopee.py.

userStories.title: Wrap logistics fetch in try/except + add platform to debug logs
userStories.description: As a system, I want logistics fetch failures to produce specific error messages, and Shopee debug logs to be distinguishable from ML debug logs.
acceptanceCriteria: In `app/services/shopee_copier.py` `copy_single_item`, wrap `_fetch_dest_logistics()` call in try/except with a specific error message: 'Falha ao buscar canais logisticos da loja destino'
On logistics fetch failure, return error result immediately (do not proceed to add_item)
In `_log_debug()`, add field `platform: 'shopee'` to the insert dict for `api_debug_logs`
Existing debug log entries from ML (in item_copier.py) are not affected
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Currently _fetch_dest_logistics is called without try/except in copy_single_item. Any exception bubbles up to the generic except handler. Adding specific handling gives better error messages. For _log_debug: check the existing api_debug_logs schema — it uses JSONB columns, so adding platform is safe. The ML copier (item_copier.py) has its own _log_debug that won't be affected.

userStories.title: Use refresh_token_expire_in from Shopee API response
userStories.description: As a system, I want to compute refresh_token_expires_at from the actual API response instead of hardcoding 30 days.
acceptanceCriteria: In `app/routers/auth_shopee.py` callback: use `token_data.get('refresh_token_expire_in')` if present to compute `refresh_expires_at`; fallback to 30 days if absent

In `app/services/shopee_api.py` `_get_token` refresh path: if refresh response returns a new `refresh_token`, also update `refresh_token_expires_at` in the DB — compute from `refresh_token_expire_in` if present, fallback to 30 days from now
Log a warning if `refresh_token_expire_in` is not present in the response
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: auth_shopee.py line 72 hardcodes `timedelta(days=30)`. The Shopee token exchange response may include `refresh_token_expire_in` (seconds). In _get_token refresh path (shopee_api.py ~line 286+), only access_token expiry is updated — refresh_token_expires_at is never recalculated even when a new refresh_token is returned.

userStories.title: Sanitize Shopee shop slugs
userStories.description: As a system, I want Shopee shop slugs to contain only safe characters [a-z0-9-] to prevent routing conflicts and injection.
acceptanceCriteria: In `app/routers/auth_shopee.py`, after generating the slug from shop_name, apply `re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9-]', '', slug)` to strip unsafe characters
If slug is empty after sanitization, fallback to `f'shop-{shop_id}'`
If slug already exists for the same org_id (UNIQUE constraint), append `-2`, `-3`, etc.
Import `re` at top of file if not already imported
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Current code at line 83: `slug = shop_name.lower().replace(' ', '-')[:50]` — allows slashes, dots, unicode. The slug is used in URL paths and DB lookups. The duplicate-suffix logic should query shopee_sellers for existing slugs in the org before inserting.

userStories.title: Create migration 013 — fix Shopee schema (FKs, indexes, unique constraints)
userStories.description: As a DBA, I want the Shopee DB schema to follow the same patterns as the ML schema for consistency and correct referential integrity.
acceptanceCriteria: Create file `app/db/migrations/013_shopee_schema_fixes.sql`
ALTER shopee_sellers FK org_id to ON DELETE CASCADE (drop and recreate FK)
ALTER shopee_copy_logs column org_id to be nullable (ALTER COLUMN org_id DROP NOT NULL)
ALTER shopee_copy_logs FK org_id to ON DELETE SET NULL (drop and recreate FK)
ALTER shopee_copy_logs FK user_id to ON DELETE SET NULL (drop and recreate FK)
DROP INDEX idx_shopee_sellers_slug_org and CREATE UNIQUE INDEX uq_shopee_sellers_slug_org ON shopee_sellers(slug, org_id)
CREATE INDEX idx_shopee_copy_logs_created_at ON shopee_copy_logs(created_at DESC)
CREATE INDEX idx_shopee_copy_logs_source_seller ON shopee_copy_logs(source_seller)
Migration file has a comment header with description and rollback instructions
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Both tables are currently empty (0 rows confirmed). Reference ML patterns: copy_sellers.org_id uses CASCADE, copy_logs.org_id uses SET NULL, copy_logs.user_id uses SET NULL. To find existing FK constraint names, the migration should use: ALTER TABLE ... DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS ... then ADD CONSTRAINT. Check migration 007_multi_tenant.sql and 008_backfill_org_data.sql for ML FK patterns. Apply via mcp__supabase__apply_migration. Supabase project ID: wrbrbhuhsaaupqsimkqz.

userStories.title: Implement rate limiting with exponential backoff in Shopee API client
userStories.description: As a system, I want Shopee API calls to automatically retry with backoff on error.too_fast, so bulk copy operations don't fail from rate limiting.
acceptanceCriteria: In `app/services/shopee_api.py`, implement retry logic in `_shop_get()` and `_shop_post()` that detects `error` field containing 'too_fast' in the response
Retry with exponential backoff using existing constants: `_RATE_LIMIT_RETRIES=5` and `_RATE_LIMIT_BASE_WAIT=2` — waits: 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s, 32s
Log a warning on each retry with attempt number and wait time
After exhausting retries, raise `ShopeeApiError` as normal
Also apply the same retry logic in `upload_image()`
The `_raise_for_shopee` check must happen AFTER the retry loop (only on final failure)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Constants at lines 26-27 are already defined but never used. The pattern should be: call API -> check if response has error.too_fast -> if yes, sleep and retry -> if no, proceed to _raise_for_shopee or return. Don't retry on non-rate-limit errors. Match the pattern from ML's item_copier.py exponential backoff on 429s.

userStories.title: Create reusable httpx.AsyncClient for Shopee API
userStories.description: As a system, I want to reuse HTTP connections across Shopee API calls to reduce latency from TCP handshake overhead.
acceptanceCriteria: In `app/services/shopee_api.py`, create a module-level lazy singleton `_http_client: httpx.AsyncClient | None = None`
Create `_get_client() -> httpx.AsyncClient` that initializes the client on first call with `limits=httpx.Limits(max_connections=20, max_keepalive_connections=10)` and `timeout=60.0`
Replace all `async with httpx.AsyncClient(...) as client:` blocks in `_shop_get`, `_shop_post`, and `upload_image` to use `_get_client()` instead
Add `async def close_client()` function that closes the client if it exists (for graceful shutdown)
In `app/main.py`, register `close_client` as a shutdown event handler: `@app.on_event('shutdown')` or via lifespan
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Currently every API call creates and destroys an httpx.AsyncClient (lines 167, 194, 326, 354, 450). This means no connection pooling. A singleton client will reuse TCP connections. Check how main.py handles lifecycle events — it may use lifespan context manager. Keep timeout=60.0 for upload_image specifically (can be overridden per-call).

userStories.title: Parallelize Shopee image uploads with semaphore
userStories.description: As a system, I want to upload images concurrently (max 3 at a time) to reduce copy time for items with many images.
acceptanceCriteria: In `app/services/shopee_copier.py`, modify `_upload_images()` to use `asyncio.gather()` with `asyncio.Semaphore(3)` for concurrent uploads
Each image still has 1 retry on failure (existing behavior preserved)
The ORDER of returned image_ids matches the order of input URLs (first image = cover photo)
Failed individual uploads return None and are filtered out (existing behavior)
Log line indicates how many images uploaded successfully vs total attempted
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Current code at lines 60-92 uploads sequentially in a for loop. For 9 images at ~2s each = 18s. With semaphore(3) = ~6s. Use asyncio.gather with return_exceptions=False. Create a helper `_upload_one(sem, shop_id, url, org_id)` that acquires semaphore, calls _upload_single_image, returns image_id or None. Then gather all helpers and filter None values. CRITICAL: preserve order — asyncio.gather preserves input order.

userStories.title: Add CSRF protection to Shopee OAuth flow
userStories.description: As a system, I want the OAuth state parameter to be cryptographically signed and single-use, preventing attackers from connecting shops to arbitrary orgs.
acceptanceCriteria: In `app/routers/auth_shopee.py` install endpoint: generate a CSRF token using `hmac.new(settings.shopee_partner_key.encode(), f'{org_id}:{ts}'.encode(), 'sha256').hexdigest()[:16]`
Encode state as `org_{org_id}_{token}_{ts}` in the redirect URI
In callback: parse state to extract org_id, token, and timestamp
Validate token by recomputing HMAC and comparing
Reject if timestamp is older than 600 seconds (10 minutes)
Return HTTP 400 with clear error message on invalid/expired state
Import `hmac` and `time` at top of file
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Current code at lines 33-36 encodes state as `org_{org_id}` with no signing. The callback at lines 49-52 trusts it blindly. Using HMAC with the partner_key (already available) avoids needing a DB table or cache for state storage. The timestamp in the state enables expiry validation. The token is truncated to 16 chars for URL friendliness. This is stateless CSRF protection — no server-side storage needed.

userStories.title: Cache logistics per destination shop in copy operations
userStories.description: As a system, I want to fetch logistics channels once per destination shop per copy operation, not once per (item, destination) pair.
acceptanceCriteria: In `app/services/shopee_copier.py`, in `copy_items()`, before the item loop: pre-fetch logistics for each unique dest_shop_id and store in a dict `logistics_cache: dict[int, list]`
Pass the pre-fetched logistics to `copy_single_item()` via a new optional parameter `logistics: list[dict] | None = None`
In `copy_single_item()`: if `logistics` is provided, skip `_fetch_dest_logistics()` call; otherwise fetch (backward compat)
Apply same change in `copy_with_dimensions()`
Net reduction: N items x M destinations goes from N*M logistics calls to M calls
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Currently _fetch_dest_logistics is called inside copy_single_item for every (item, dest) pair. For 10 items x 3 dests = 30 calls, but only 3 are unique. Pre-fetch before the loop. Use try/except on pre-fetch — if logistics fetch fails for a dest, log error and skip that dest entirely.

userStories.title: Reuse source item data across destination shops
userStories.description: As a system, I want to fetch source item data once per item and reuse it for all destination shops, instead of re-fetching for each destination.
acceptanceCriteria: In `app/services/shopee_copier.py`, in `copy_items()`, before the dest-shop inner loop: pre-fetch source item via `_fetch_source_item()` and store result
Pass pre-fetched source data to `copy_single_item()` via a new optional parameter `source_data: dict | None = None`
In `copy_single_item()`: if `source_data` is provided, skip `_fetch_source_item()` call; otherwise fetch (backward compat)
Apply same change in `copy_with_dimensions()`
Net reduction: 1 item x N destinations goes from 3*N API calls to 3 calls
If source fetch fails, skip all destinations for that item with error logged
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Currently copy_single_item fetches source data (base_info + extra_info + models = 3 API calls) every time it's called. For 1 item copied to 3 destinations = 9 redundant calls. Pre-fetch in the outer loop of copy_items. The source_data dict contains keys like item_name, description, images, etc. — check _fetch_source_item return format.

userStories.title: DRY refactor — unify copy_items and copy_with_dimensions
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to eliminate the ~90% code duplication between the two copy functions by extracting shared logic.
acceptanceCriteria: Create internal function `async _run_copy_job(source_shop_id, item_ids, dest_shop_ids, org_id, user_id, log_id, dimensions=None, logistics_cache=None, source_data_cache=None) -> list[dict]` that contains the shared copy loop logic
`copy_items()` becomes a thin wrapper that resolves slugs, creates log entry, calls `_run_copy_job`, updates log
`copy_with_dimensions()` becomes a thin wrapper that resolves slugs, creates log entry, calls `_run_copy_job` with dimensions, updates log
Trial accounting uses the same pattern in both wrappers (pre-increment + refund)
All existing behavior preserved — no functional regression
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: copy_items and copy_with_dimensions are ~90% identical. The differences: copy_items handles multiple item_ids, copy_with_dimensions passes dimensions. Both do: resolve slugs -> create log -> loop items x dests -> update log. After US-013 and US-014 added the caching params, the refactor is even cleaner. Careful: the trial accounting differs — copy_items pre-increments, copy_with_dimensions doesn't. Unify to pre-increment pattern.

userStories.title: Add init_tier_variation and add_model API methods
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Shopee API wrapper functions for tier variation initialization and model creation so the copier can create products with variations.
acceptanceCriteria: In `app/services/shopee_api.py`, add `async def init_tier_variation(shop_id: int, item_id: int, tier_variation: list[dict], org_id: str) -> dict` that POSTs to `/api/v2/product/init_tier_variation`
Add `async def add_model(shop_id: int, item_id: int, model_list: list[dict], org_id: str) -> dict` that POSTs to `/api/v2/product/add_model`
Both functions use the existing `_shop_post` helper for signing and auth
Both functions include proper docstrings describing the payload format
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: These are the two API calls needed after add_item to set up variations. init_tier_variation defines the tier structure (e.g., Color, Size). add_model defines the specific combinations with prices and stock. Payload formats: init_tier_variation body: {item_id, tier_variation: [{name: 'Color', option_list: [{option: 'Red', image: {image_id: '...'}}]}]}. add_model body: {item_id, model_list: [{tier_index: [0, 0], normal_stock: 10, original_price: 99.9, model_sku: 'SKU-RED-S'}]}. Use _shop_post which handles HMAC signing.

userStories.title: Implement variation copy flow in shopee_copier
userStories.description: As an operator, I want products with variations (size, color) to be copied with all their models, prices, and individual stock.
acceptanceCriteria: In `app/services/shopee_copier.py`, in `_build_shopee_payload`: if source has `has_model: True`, exclude `normal_stock` from the add_item payload
After successful `add_item` in `copy_single_item`: if source has models data (from `_fetch_source_item`), call `init_tier_variation` with tier names and options extracted from source models
After `init_tier_variation`: call `add_model` with model list containing per-model prices, stock, and SKUs
If tier variation images exist, upload them before calling init_tier_variation and include image_ids
If `init_tier_variation` or `add_model` fails, the item is still created (mark as 'partial' in result, log the variation error)
Result dict includes `models_copied: int` and `models_total: int` fields
Items without models (has_model: False) continue to work exactly as before
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the core variation feature. The source data from _fetch_source_item already includes 'models' key from get_model_list. Structure: models response has 'tier_variation' (tier definitions) and 'model' (specific combinations). Steps: (1) check has_model from base_info, (2) extract tier_variation from models data — each tier has name + option_list, (3) upload tier images if present, (4) call init_tier_variation, (5) build model_list from models data — each model has tier_index, price, stock, sku, (6) call add_model. Handle the case where models data is empty/None (fallback to simple item). Mark result as 'partial' if item created but variations failed.

userStories.title: Fix Shopee retry-dimensions payload in CopyPage frontend
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the 'Informar dimensoes' retry button to work correctly for Shopee copies, sending the required source/destinations/item_id fields.
acceptanceCriteria: In `frontend/src/pages/CopyPage.tsx`, modify `handleLogRetry` for platform 'shopee' to send `{ source: log.source_seller, destinations: log.dest_sellers, item_id: String(log.source_item_id), dimensions: dims }` instead of `{ log_id, dimensions }`
The `onRetrySubmit` callback receives the full log object (not just logId) to access source_seller, dest_sellers, source_item_id
ML retry continues to send `{ log_id, dimensions }` unchanged
The CopyLog type in `frontend/src/lib/api.ts` includes `source_seller: string`, `dest_sellers: string[]`, `source_item_id: string | number` fields
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Critical frontend bug. Line 192 sends { log_id, dimensions } for Shopee but the backend ShopeeCopyWithDimensionsRequest expects { source, destinations, item_id, dimensions }. The orphaned ShopeeCopyPage.tsx (lines 315-329) has the correct implementation — use it as reference. The log object from /api/shopee/copy/logs contains source_seller, dest_sellers, and source_item_id. The onRetrySubmit callback chain passes through LogCard — may need to pass the full log object instead of just logId.

userStories.title: Show weight and description status in Shopee preview
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to see the item weight and whether it has a description in the preview, so I know before copying if dimensions will be needed.
acceptanceCriteria: In `frontend/src/lib/api.ts`, extend `ItemPreview` type with optional fields: `weight?: number`, `has_description?: boolean`, `stock?: number`
In `frontend/src/pages/CopyPage.tsx` `handlePreview`, when normalizing Shopee preview data, include `weight`, `has_description`, and `stock` from the Shopee response
In the preview card rendering section, show weight (in kg) when available
Show a warning indicator when `has_description` is false (Shopee requires description)
ML preview is not affected (fields are optional)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: CopyPage.handlePreview (lines 154-162) normalizes Shopee data but drops weight, has_description, stock. The orphaned ShopeeCopyPage (lines 665-683) shows weight correctly as '{p.weight}kg' — use as reference. The backend /api/shopee/copy/preview/{item_id} already returns these fields.

userStories.title: Fix resolve-sellers error handling for Shopee in CopyForm
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to see error messages when Shopee seller resolution fails, instead of silent failure.
acceptanceCriteria: In `frontend/src/components/CopyForm.tsx`, in the Shopee resolve-sellers path: if `!res.ok`, parse the error response and show it via toast
If the user has no Shopee sellers connected but pasted a Shopee URL, show specific message: 'Nenhuma loja Shopee conectada'
Loading state is properly reset on error (setResolving(false) or equivalent)
ML resolve path is not affected
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: CopyForm.tsx line ~198: `if (!res.ok) return;` silently ignores Shopee resolve errors. The ML path shows errors properly. Add toast(err.detail, 'error') matching the ML pattern. For the 'no Shopee sellers' case: check if shopeeSellers array is empty before even calling resolve.

userStories.title: Add Shopee connect option to empty state and QuickStartGuide
userStories.description: As an admin of a new org, I want to see the option to connect Shopee on the onboarding screen, so Shopee-only orgs have a guided path.
acceptanceCriteria: In `frontend/src/App.tsx`, in the empty state section (where 'Conectar conta do Mercado Livre' button is shown): add a 'Conectar loja Shopee' button that calls `/api/shopee/install`
The Shopee button has consistent visual styling with the ML button (side by side or stacked)
QuickStartGuide section mentions Shopee as a connection option alongside ML
Paywall feature list (lines ~308-323) includes a Shopee-related feature mention
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: App.tsx lines 418-491 show the empty state with only ML connect button. Lines 748-833 show QuickStartGuide mentioning only ML. The Shopee install flow uses the same pattern as ML: fetch /api/shopee/install, get redirect_url, window.location.href = redirect_url. Look at how the ML connect button works and replicate the pattern.

userStories.title: Delete ShopeeCopyPage.tsx (dead code)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to remove the orphaned ShopeeCopyPage that is never imported or rendered, to reduce codebase size and confusion.
acceptanceCriteria: Delete file `frontend/src/pages/ShopeeCopyPage.tsx`
Verify no file in `frontend/src/` imports ShopeeCopyPage (grep for 'ShopeeCopyPage')
Build passes: `cd frontend && npm run build` (or typecheck equivalent)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This file is ~950 lines that are never rendered. All useful logic has been extracted to CopyPage in previous stories (US-018 for retry payload, US-019 for preview weight). App.tsx never imports ShopeeCopyPage. Safe to delete. Run grep to triple-check no references exist.

userStories.title: Extract shared components — StatusBadge, isDimensionError
userStories.description: As a developer, I want duplicated frontend components and helpers extracted to shared files for maintainability.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `frontend/src/components/StatusBadge.tsx` with the StatusBadge component extracted from CopyPage (consolidating the identical implementations)
Create `frontend/src/lib/helpers.ts` with `isDimensionError(error: string): boolean` that includes both ML dimension keywords AND Shopee 'weight' keyword check
Update `frontend/src/pages/CopyPage.tsx` to import StatusBadge from the shared component and isDimensionError from helpers
Remove the local StatusBadge and isDimensionError definitions from CopyPage
Remove dead types from `frontend/src/lib/api.ts`: `ShopeeCopyResponse`, `ShopeeCopyResult` (never imported anywhere)
Build passes without errors
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: StatusBadge is duplicated in CopyPage (lines ~563-578). isDimensionError is in CopyPage (lines ~19-27) missing the 'weight' check that the orphaned ShopeeCopyPage had. ShopeeCopyResponse/ShopeeCopyResult in api.ts (lines 117-132) are defined but never imported. After US-022 deletes ShopeeCopyPage, only CopyPage remains as the consumer.
